title: 5 Manual Processes Costing Your Construction Company 10+ Hours a Week
subtitle: A 30-minute self-audit to spot where your team is bleeding time and what to fix first

sections.title: Document Signing & Certification
sections.description: PDFs signed page-by-page with certificates and passwords are a hidden tax on your authorized signers and a compliance risk when pages get skipped.

items.text: Audit how many PDFs your team signs page-by-page in Adobe each day
items.tip: Track one signer for 48 hours and count documents plus pages per document

items.text: Identify every authorized signer handling 10+ multi-page documents daily
items.tip: Owners and ops leads are usually the bottleneck here

items.text: Pull the last 30 days of signed documents and flag any with skipped pages or missing certs
items.tip: Compliance risk compounds fast on SWPPPs and debit notes

items.text: Set up a watched folder that auto-signs designated pages and routes completed PDFs for one-click approval
items.tip: Replace 200 clicks per batch with a single review-and-approve step

sections.title: Job Order & Project Data Entry
sections.description: The same project info gets retyped into PM tools, Trello cards, job orders, and bid documents every time a new job opens.

items.text: List every system where new job data is entered manually (PM tool, Trello, job orders, bid docs)
items.tip: Most teams find 4-6 duplicate entry points

items.text: Create one intake form that captures project description, area, address, and client once
items.tip: Use a simple web form or ClickUp template as the source of truth

items.text: Connect the intake form to all downstream tools so data populates automatically
items.tip: n8n or Zapier can fan out one record to every system in under a minute

sections.title: Client & Subcontractor Communication
sections.description: Status updates, change orders, and schedule changes get retyped across email, text, and phone calls to every stakeholder.

items.text: Map which clients and subs get updates by email, text, or call and how often
items.tip: Look for the same message sent three different ways

items.text: Build status update templates triggered by project milestones in your PM tool
items.tip: Auto-send when a stage moves from In Progress to Complete

items.text: Centralize all client and sub communications into one threaded log per project
items.tip: Eliminates the "who told who what" problem during disputes

sections.title: Invoicing & Payment Tracking
sections.description: Pulling hours from timesheets, matching them to jobs, generating invoices, and chasing payments is usually a multi-day manual loop.

items.text: Map the steps from timesheet entry to invoice sent and time each one
items.tip: Most companies lose 4-6 hours per week here

items.text: Connect timesheets and job costs directly to invoice generation in QuickBooks or Xero
items.tip: Eliminates the spreadsheet middle step entirely

items.text: Set automated payment reminders at 7, 14, and 30 days past due
items.tip: Recovers $5,000-$15,000 in late receivables for most mid-size GCs

sections.title: Reporting & Compliance Documentation
sections.description: Weekly progress reports, safety logs, and compliance docs get assembled by hand from photos, notes, and field updates every Friday.

items.text: List every recurring report your team builds manually each week or month
items.tip: Safety logs, progress reports, and SWPPP docs are the usual suspects

items.text: Standardize field data capture with a mobile form crews fill out daily
items.tip: One photo plus three fields beats end-of-week reconstruction

items.text: Auto-assemble weekly reports from field data into a branded PDF ready for client delivery
items.tip: Friday afternoon disappears as a reporting day

completion_cta.headline: Found 3+ checked boxes? You're losing 10+ hours a week.
completion_cta.description: Book a free 30-minute automation audit and I'll map the highest-ROI fix for your top section, with a clear cost and timeline. No pitch, just the plan.
